vulnerability.title: Configure Time Service Maxpoll Interval
vulnerability.description: The maxpoll should be configured to in /etc/ntp.conf or /etc/chrony.conf (or /etc/chrony.d/) to continuously poll time servers. To configure maxpoll in /etc/ntp.conf or /etc/chrony.conf (or /etc/chrony.d/) add the following after each server, pool or peer entry: maxpoll to server directives. If using chrony, any pool directives should be configured too.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( rpm --quiet -q chrony || rpm --quiet -q ntp ); }; then var_time_service_set_maxpoll='' pof="/usr/sbin/pidof" CONFIG_FILES="/etc/ntp.conf" $pof ntpd || { CHRONY_D_PATH=/etc/chrony.d/ mapfile -t CONFIG_FILES < <(find ${CHRONY_D_PATH}.* -type f -name '*.conf') CONFIG_FILES+=(/etc/chrony.conf) } # get list of ntp files for config_file in "${CONFIG_FILES[@]}" ; do # Set maxpoll values to var_time_service_set_maxpoll sed -i "s/^\(\(server\|pool\|peer\).*maxpoll\) [0-9,-][0-9]*\(.*\)$/\1 $var_time_service_set_maxpoll \3/" "$config_file" done for config_file in "${CONFIG_FILES[@]}" ; do # Add maxpoll to server, pool or peer entries without maxpoll grep "^\(server\|pool\|peer\)" "$config_file" | grep -v maxpo

triage_decision.reason: The remediation involves editing a standard configuration file for the NTP/chrony service to set the maxpoll interval, a routine change that does not affect authentication, critical services, or system boot. It is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; Incorrect maxpoll value could lead to time drift or increased network traffic

execution_details.stdout:
# Use public servers from the pool.ntp.org project.
# Please consider joining the pool (https://www.pool.ntp.org/join.html).
#pool 2.rocky.pool.ntp.org iburst maxpoll 16

# Use NTP servers from DHCP.
sourcedir /run/chrony-dhcp

# Record the rate at which the system clock gains/losses time.
driftfile /var/lib/chrony/drift

# Allow the system clock to be stepped in the first three updates
# if its offset is larger than 1 second.
makestep 1.0 3

# Enable kernel synchronization of the real-time clock (RTC).
rtcsync

# Enable hardware timestamping on all interfaces that support it.
#hwtimestamp *

# Increase the minimum number of selectable sources required to adjust
# the system clock.
#minsources 2

# Allow NTP client access from local network.
#allow 192.168.0.0/16

# Serve time even if not synchronized to a time source.
#local stratum 10

# Require authentication (nts or key option) for all NTP sources.
#authselectmode require

# Specify file containing keys for NTP authentication.
keyfile /etc/chrony.keys

# Save NTS keys and cookies.
ntsdumpdir /var/lib/chrony

# Insert/delete leap seconds by slewing instead of stepping.
#leapsecmode slew

# Get TAI-UTC offset and leap seconds from the system tz database.
leapsectz right/UTC

# Specify directory for log files.
logdir /var/log/chrony

# Select which information is logged.
#log measurements statistics tracking
# Per : Set port 0 in /etc/chrony.conf
port 0
# Per : Set cmdport 0 in /etc/chrony.conf
cmdport 0
server 127.0.0.1 iburst

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan addresses the core requirement of ensuring a maxpoll of 10 on all server/pool/peer directives, which will resolve the vulnerability if executed correctly. However, the sed command needs refinement for reliability, and the script should handle additional configuration directories and validate service configuration before restart.
concerns: Sed expression may not match all valid line formats (e.g., variable whitespace, tabs, multiple options or commented lines).
The proposed command lumps replacement and append logic into a single complex expression; clearer separate steps are safer.
Only /etc/chrony.conf and /etc/ntp.conf are modified – chrony.d directories, if present, are ignored.
There is no backup of original configuration files before editing, risking accidental loss of custom settings.
Service restart is unconditional; if the service fails to start due to syntax errors, the system could lose time synchronization.
The plan does not verify that the new value is accepted by the daemon (no ntp/chrony configuration check).
suggested_improvements: Write two separate, clearer sed commands: one to replace existing maxpoll, one to append if it does not exist; include tests for comment and whitespace variations.
Add a step to backup each configuration file before modification (e.g., cp -p /etc/chrony.conf /etc/chrony.conf.bak).
Detect and iterate over any chrony.d/*.conf files and apply the same changes.
After editing, run `chronyc sources -v` or `ntpq -p` in test mode or use the service's config-check option (e.g., `chronyd -t` or `ntp -t`) before restarting to ensure the config is valid.
Handle service name variations (chronyd vs chronyd, ntpd vs ntpd) and fail gracefully if the service is not active.
Add idempotency checks: if maxpoll already set to 10, skip editing that line to avoid unnecessary restarts.
